text:
*[Bug 19032] New: SETVAR does not prime the smallcache binding cache*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19032>

            Bug ID: 19032
           Summary: SETVAR does not prime the smallcache binding cache
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Other
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Low-level
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3646
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3646&action=edit>
Patch.

GETVAR has a fallback to GET_BINDING_CELL_CACHE when the smallcache slot is
R_NilValue (eval.c line 5896), but SETVAR lacks the equivalent. This means
SETVAR relies on a prior GETVAR to prime the cache. When no GETVAR has done so,
every SETVAR falls through to defineVar.

The patch adds the same R_NilValue fallback to SETVAR's smallcache path, and a
SET_CACHED_BINDING call after the defineVar fallback to prime the cache on
first binding creation.

This gives a small but meaningful optimization for loop bodies of the form:

```
for (i in 1:n) {

  # assign variables (SETVAR before GETVAR)
  x <- 1
  y <- 2

  # use variables (GETVAR)
  z <- x + y

}
```

*[Bug 19033] New: text Chinese with srt gives larger size of character*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19033>

            Bug ID: 19033
           Summary: text Chinese with srt gives larger size of character
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: x86_64/x64/amd64 (64-bit)
                OS: Windows 64-bit
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Graphics
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:jszhao@yeah.net|jszhao@yeah.net>

Created attachment 3648
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3648&action=edit>
srt in  text() cause larger size of Chinese characters

Hi there,

In the following code, you'll notice that when srt is not 0 or a multiple of 90
(e.g., 45), Chinese characters rendered by text() appear significantly larger.
English characters are unaffected.

```
#png("abc.png", width = 7*600, height=7*600, res = 600, pointsize = 10)
plot(1:5, 1:5, type = "n")
text(2,2, "中文 English srt = 0", srt = 0, pos = 1)
text(2,2, "中文 English srt = 180", srt = 180, pos = 3)
text(3,3, "中文 English srt = 45", srt = 45)
text(3,4, "中文 English srt = 15", srt = 15)
text(4,4, "中文 English srt = 90", srt = 90, pos = 2)
text(4,4, "中文 English srt = -90", srt = -90, pos = 4)
#dev.off()
```

```
> sessionInfo()
R version 4.5.3 (2026-03-11 ucrt)
Platform: x86_64-w64-mingw32/x64
Running under: Windows 10 x64 (build 19045)

Matrix products: default
  LAPACK version 3.12.1

locale:
[1] LC_COLLATE=Chinese (Simplified)_China.utf8 
[2] LC_CTYPE=Chinese (Simplified)_China.utf8   
[3] LC_MONETARY=Chinese (Simplified)_China.utf8
[4] LC_NUMERIC=C                               
[5] LC_TIME=Chinese (Simplified)_China.utf8    

time zone: Asia/Shanghai
tzcode source: internal

attached base packages:
[1] stats     graphics  grDevices utils     datasets  methods   base     

loaded via a namespace (and not attached):
[1] compiler_4.5.3
```

*[Bug 19034] New: R fails to install packages with "Error in if (custom.bin) { : argument is of length zero" (possibly under tools:::.install_packages)*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19034>

            Bug ID: 19034
           Summary: R fails to install packages with "Error in if
                    (custom.bin) { : argument is of length zero" (possibly
                    under tools:::.install_packages)
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: arm64/M1
                OS: macOS
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Mac GUI / Mac specific
          Assignee: <mailto:simon.urbanek@r-project.org|simon.urbanek@r-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:g.vegayon@gmail.com|g.vegayon@gmail.com>
                CC: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>

Created attachment 3649
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3649&action=edit>
Log installation from epiworldR

## Description

I have faced this issue on GitHub actions with a couple of packages already.
These happened on using
[`rhub/actions/r-check@v1`](<https://github.com/UofUEpiBio/epiworldR/actions/runs/23613653084/job/68778498169>)
and
[`r-lib/actions/r-setup-dependencies@v2`](<https://github.com/USCCANA/netdiffuseR/actions/runs/23659778933/job/68926663664?pr=74>)
using R-devel on MacOS arm64. These are the last few lines I got from each:

rhub/actions


  ** R
  ** inst
  ** byte-compile and prepare package for lazy loading
  ** help
  *** installing help indices
  *** copying figures
  ** building package indices
  ** installing vignettes
  ** testing if installed package can be loaded from temporary location
  ** checking absolute paths in shared objects and dynamic libraries
  ** testing if installed package can be loaded from final location
  ** testing if installed package keeps a record of temporary installation path
  * SHA256 sums
  * creating tarball
  Error in if (custom.bin) { : argument is of length zero
  * removing
‘/Users/runner/work/epiworldR/epiworldR/check/epiworldR.Rcheck/epiworldR’


r-lib/actions


  ** R
  ** byte-compile and prepare package for lazy loading
  ** help
  *** installing help indices
  ** building package indices
  ** testing if installed package can be loaded
  * SHA256 sums
  * creating tarball
  Error: Error in if (custom.bin) { : argument is of length zero

## Location

I have tracked down the message to the `tools:::.install.packages()` function
[here](<https://github.com/r-devel/r-svn/blob/27f30b343dc2fc2c6c889c949a0a24ca0f4278b3/src/library/tools/R/install.R#L469-L470>):

  custom.bin <- .pkg.type(.Platform$pkgType) == "other.binary"
  filename <- if (custom.bin) {

Usure what could be causing it. I'm attaching the installation log from
`epiworldR`. The log for netdiffuseR is available on the link.

text:
*[Bug 19035] New: postscript(): PS_Open() is too pessimistic wrt R_popen(), failing in Docker on GitHub Actions*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19035>

            Bug ID: 19035
           Summary: postscript(): PS_Open() is too pessimistic wrt
                    R_popen(), failing in Docker on GitHub Actions
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: All
                OS: Linux
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Graphics
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>

Created attachment 3650
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3650&action=edit>
Suggested fix

Originally [discovered by Jeroen
Ooms](<https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-package-devel/2026q1/012328.html>):

In certain Linux container environments (e.g. Docker on GitHub Actions) the
`clone3()` system call doesn't work (fails with `ENOSYS`). `libc` is ready for
that and retries the operation with a call to `clone()` instead. With certain
`libc` versions (e.g. `libc6` version 2.42-13 in current Debian testing), when
`popen` first calls `clone3()`, sees it fail, retries with `clone()`, and then
succeeds, `errno` is non-zero upon return despite the `FILE*` has been
successfully opened. `PS_Open()` considers such an `R_popen()` call to fail
because it checks for a non-zero `errno` separately from a `NULL` return value.
This causes the `bitmap()` graphical device to fail:

```
$ Rscript -e 'bitmap("/dev/null")'
$ strace -f -o /dev/null --fault=clone3 Rscript -e 'bitmap("/dev/null")'
Error in postscript(file = cmd, width = width, height = height, pointsize =
pointsize,  : 
  cannot open 'postscript' pipe to ''/usr/bin/gs' -dNOPAUSE -dBATCH -q
-sDEVICE=png16m -r72 -dAutoRotatePages=/None -g504x504 -sOutputFile='/dev/null'
-'
Calls: bitmap -> postscript
Execution halted
```

```
$ R -d gdb
...
(gdb) b PS_Open
(gdb) c
> bitmap("/dev/null")
Breakpoint 1, PS_Open (dd=dd@entry=0x558f7a8b9f80, pd=pd@entry=0x558f7a8bb0a0)
at ./src/library/grDevices/src/devPS.c:3723
3723    {
(gdb) tcatch syscall clone3
Catchpoint 2 (syscall 'clone3' [435])
(gdb) c
Temporary catchpoint 2 (call to syscall clone3), __GI___clone3 () at
../sysdeps/unix/sysv/linux/x86_64/clone3.S:62
62              test    %RAX_LP, %RAX_LP
# poor man's fault injection: jump over the syscall instruction, making it look
like it failed
(gdb) tbreak *$pc
(gdb) jump *$pc
(gdb) fini
#0  0x00007f86a9a18b4b in __spawnix (pid=pid@entry=0x5565da305c50,
file=file@entry=0x7f86a9ac6ea4 "/bin/sh",
file_actions=file_actions@entry=0x7ffc9fe71e10, 
    attrp=attrp@entry=0x0, argv=argv@entry=0x7ffc9fe71de0, envp=0x5565d77dc120,
xflags=0, exec=0x7f86a99fd090 <__GI_execve>) at
../sysdeps/unix/sysv/linux/spawni.c:421
421       new_pid = __clone3 (&clone_args, sizeof (clone_args), __spawni_child,
(gdb) n
424       if (new_pid == -1 && (errno == ENOSYS || errno == EINVAL))
(gdb) p new_pid
$1 = -1
(gdb) p errno
$2 = 38 # <-- ENOSYS
...
(gdb) n
427           args.use_clone3 = false;
...
429             new_pid = __clone_internal_fallback (&clone_args,
__spawni_child,
...
# eventually:
PS_Open (dd=dd@entry=0x555d3dedaf80, pd=pd@entry=0x555d3df86340) at
./src/library/grDevices/src/devPS.c:3745
3745            if (!pd->psfp || errno != 0) {
(gdb) p pd->psfp
$18 = (FILE *) 0x555d3df2c9f0 # popen() succeeded...
(gdb) p errno
$19 = 38 # ...but with a non-zero errno...
# so R calls error(...)
```

This failure is not reproducible on an older system (Debian oldstable).
Apparently, `popen` used to set `errno` to 0 even if `clone3` initially failed.
Technically, neither [Linux
manpages](<https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man3/popen.3.html>) nor
[POSIX](<https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9799919799/functions/popen.html>)
say what `errno` is going to be set to upon success. The suggested fix is to

pointsize,  : 
  cannot open 'postscript' pipe to ''/usr/bin/gs' -dNOPAUSE -dBATCH -q
-sDEVICE=png16m -r72 -dAutoRotatePages=/None -g504x504 -sOutputFile='/dev/null'

$ R -d gdb

(gdb) c
> bitmap("/dev/null")
Breakpoint 1, PS_Open (dd=dd@entry=0x558f7a8b9f80, pd=pd@entry=0x558f7a8bb0a0)
at ./src/library/grDevices/src/devPS.c:3723
3723    {
(gdb) tcatch syscall clone3
Catchpoint 2 (syscall 'clone3' [435])
(gdb) c
Temporary catchpoint 2 (call to syscall clone3), __GI___clone3 () at
../sysdeps/unix/sysv/linux/x86_64/clone3.S:62
62              test    %RAX_LP, %RAX_LP
# poor man's fault injection: jump over the syscall instruction, making it look
like it failed
(gdb) tbreak *$pc
(gdb) jump *$pc
(gdb) fini
#0  0x00007f86a9a18b4b in __spawnix (pid=pid@entry=0x5565da305c50,
file=file@entry=0x7f86a9ac6ea4 "/bin/sh",
file_actions=file_actions@entry=0x7ffc9fe71e10, 
    attrp=attrp@entry=0x0, argv=argv@entry=0x7ffc9fe71de0, envp=0x5565d77dc120,
xflags=0, exec=0x7f86a99fd090 <__GI_execve>) at
../sysdeps/unix/sysv/linux/spawni.c:421
421       new_pid = __clone3 (&clone_args, sizeof (clone_args), __spawni_child,
(gdb) n
424       if (new_pid == -1 && (errno == ENOSYS || errno == EINVAL))
(gdb) p new_pid
$1 = -1
(gdb) p errno
$2 = 38 # <-- ENOSYS
...
(gdb) n
427           args.use_clone3 = false;
...
429             new_pid = __clone_internal_fallback (&clone_args,
__spawni_child,
...
# eventually:
PS_Open (dd=dd@entry=0x555d3dedaf80, pd=pd@entry=0x555d3df86340) at
./src/library/grDevices/src/devPS.c:3745
3745            if (!pd->psfp || errno != 0) {
(gdb) p pd->psfp
$18 = (FILE *) 0x555d3df2c9f0 # popen() succeeded...
(gdb) p errno
$19 = 38 # ...but with a non-zero errno...
# so R calls error(...)

This failure is not reproducible on an older system (Debian oldstable).
Apparently,
is going to be set to upon success. The suggested fix is to

text:
only look at `pd->psfp` when trying to determine whether `R_popen()` succeeded.
Patched R tested with and without `strace -f -o /dev/null --fault=clone3` on
Debian testing.

elements.text:
succeeded.
Patched R tested with and without

text:
*[Bug 18035] a bug in wilcox.test() caused by precision error when there are ties*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18035>

Michael Chirico (<mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |michaelchirico4@gmail.com

--- Comment #4 from Michael Chirico (<mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>) ---
I agree with Elin that this is working exactly as documented. I am sympathetic
to the argument that a more "natural" default for `digits.rank` might be
advisable. In this case even `digits.rank=14` would have given the expected
result:

```
data.frame(
  digits = 0:20,
  V = sapply(0:20, \(dig) unname(wilcox.test(after-before,
digits.rank=dig)$statistic))
)
#    digits   V
# 1       0 6.5
# 2       1 6.5
# 3       2 6.5
# 4       3 6.5
# 5       4 6.5
# 6       5 6.5
# 7       6 6.5
# 8       7 6.5
# 9       8 6.5
# 10      9 6.5
# 11     10 6.5
# 12     11 6.5
# 13     12 6.5
# 14     13 6.5
# 15     14 6.0
# 16     15 5.5
# 17     16 5.5
# 18     17 5.5
# 19     18 5.5
# 20     19 5.5
# 21     20 5.5
```

With regards to back-compatibility, my suspicion is any analysis that differs
substantially between `digits.rank=Inf` and `digits.rank=10L` is "more correct"
from the analyst's point-of-view for the latter choice, but it would be worth
evaluating on CRAN packages.

There are not too many packages using it on CRAN, only about 100:
<https://github.com/search?q=org%3Acran+%2Fwilcox%5C.test%5C%28%2F&type=code>

If such an analysis is of interest I can have a go at it.
